subject: Statistics
title: What Is a P-Value?
description: A fast breakdown of What Is a P-Value? for builders.

hook.text: The p-value is the most used and most misunderstood number in all of science.

buildup.text: It answers one question: if the null is true, how likely is data this extreme or more extreme?

discovery.text: A p-value of 0.03 means there's a 3% chance of seeing data this extreme if nothing is really happening.

twist.text: The p-value is NOT the probability the hypothesis is true. That's the most common misinterpretation in science.

climax.text: Small p-value: the data is surprising under the null. That's it. No more, no less.

quiz.question: What does a p-value of 0.03 actually mean?
options: If the null hypothesis were true, there's a 3% chance of seeing data this extreme
There's a 3% chance the null hypothesis is true
The treatment works 97% of the time
